mappings.rationaleAI: Singapore's British colonial rule, Japanese occupation, federation with Malaysia, and withdrawal to form an independent republic (B.2.a), and post-1965 PAP regime and rapid economic growth (B.2.c).
Bangladesh's emergence as a nation in 1971 through liberation war, Mujibur Rahman's government, martial-law administrations, and return to parliamentary system are directly covered (A.4).
Myanmar's emergence of nationalism and British response (B.1.a), postwar independence under U Nu, military government under Ne Win, and continued military repression (B.1.b).

Pakistan's national consolidation under Jinnah (A.3.a), military governments, border conflicts with India, civil war leading to Bangladesh's secession, and alternating civilian and military rule (A.3.b).

Vietnam's nationalist movements, Ho Chi Minh's government, French reconquest attempt, Geneva Accords, US military intervention, civil war, reunification, and subsequent economic reforms (B.4.a–d) are comprehensively covered.
Gandhi's satyagraha movement of nonviolent resistance, Round Table Conference, and intensified agitation for Indian independence are directly covered (A.1.a).
Malaya's British economic policies, Japanese occupation, Federation of Malaya, creation of Malaysia, and post-1965 United Malays National Organization dominance (B.2.a–b).
Thailand's post-WWI developments, constitutional monarchy, Japanese occupation (B.3.a), and post-war political instability, military domination, and return to civilian government (B.3.b).

India's independence movement and Gandhi's satyagraha (A.1.a), partition and independence (A.1.b), post-independence parliamentary system under Nehru (A.2.a), and foreign policy including Kashmir dispute and China conflict (A.2.b).
Indonesia's Dutch suppression of revolts, Japanese occupation, Sukarno's independence proclamation (C.1.a), and Guided Democracy, Suharto regime, East Timor, and democratization (C.1.b).
The Philippines' US administration, growth of nationalist parties, commonwealth status, Japanese occupation, and return of US control (C.2.a).
Mao's China and the 1962 border conflict with India and Chinese hegemony over Tibet (A.6) connect to South Asian regional dynamics.

WWII's impact covers India's wartime contributions and partition (A.1.b), Burma's Japanese occupation (B.1.a), Malaya's Japanese occupation (B.2.a), Thailand's Japanese occupation (B.3.a), French Indochina under Japanese occupation (B.4.a), Indonesia's Japanese occupation (C.1.a.i), and the Philippines' Japanese occupation (C.2.a).
British imperial governance and decolonization in India (A.1), Burma (B.1.a), and Malaya (B.2.a) are directly covered.
Cold War dynamics cover US military intervention in Vietnam (B.4.c) and the Suharto regime's mass execution of communists in Indonesia (C.1.b).
Japan's wartime occupations across Southeast Asia — Burma (B.1.a), Malaya (B.2.a), Thailand (B.3.a), Indochina (B.4.a), Indonesia (C.1.a.i), and the Philippines (C.2.a) — are directly covered.
Chinese invasion and hegemony over Tibet (A.6) and Vietnam's border war with China (B.4.d) are directly covered.
